_metadata.description: BYU-Idaho Course Design Language — six principles of design coherence (visual grammar, narrative metaphor, dual-framing, structural beats, observable rubrics, alignment traceability) plus the palette, audit tags, and cognitive-load/Hattie-phase mappings.
_metadata.owner: Chaz Clark (BYUI faculty-development; Architects of Learning course 405800)
_metadata.runtime_strategy_rationale: Body MD ~22,895 chars / 4 ≈ 5,724 tokens — within the 'either' band per runtime_strategy_rules. Set to read_at_runtime per named_override_patterns.selective_load: canvas_course_expert and canvas_content_sync load this file selectively per audit/generation task from a pool of ~12 knowledge files, not as a stable embedded prefix. Selective-load defeats prompt-cache benefits of embed.

scope: The canonical design-language specification for BYU-Idaho Canvas courses — prescriptive principles for whether a course feels like one coherent artifact to the student. Boundaries: does not cover Hattie phase progression (see hattie_3phase) or working-memory mechanics (see cognitive_load_theory); see those files plus designer_thinking, taxonomy_explorer, toyota_gap_analysis for adjacent concerns.

principles.name: Principle 1 — Unified Visual Grammar
principles.compact_statement: Every artifact is assembled from the same small set of components (banner, role chip, title, section headers, callout boxes, cards with semantic top-borders, tables with primary-color headers). Students learn the grammar once on Week 01 and get it for free for the rest of the course.
principles.trigger: Auditing any instructional artifact (week page, assignment, syllabus) for visual consistency across weeks.
principles.trust_marker: design_principle: visual_grammar — flagged when banner color doesn't match chip/title, H1 size/font varies across pages, same content type rendered different ways across weeks, decorative color use, or >6 accent colors on one page.

principles.name: Principle 2 — Sustained Narrative Metaphor
principles.compact_statement: The course is named for a metaphor and the metaphor is load-bearing, not decorative — it appears in the course title, recurring component names (Architect's Lens, Architect's Reflection, Architect's Question), the reasoning of every weekly purpose statement, and in the capstone.
principles.trigger: Auditing weekly artifacts for whether the course metaphor survives beyond syllabus marketing language into actual reasoning at the weekly and capstone level.
principles.trust_marker: design_principle: narrative_metaphor — flagged when metaphor is named in syllabus but absent from weekly pages, switches or mixes mid-course, appears in titles but not in reasoning, or disappears after Week 3-4.

principles.name: Principle 3 — Dual-Framing on Every Task
principles.compact_statement: Every instructional artifact opens with a two-panel header — one panel answers why this exists (Purpose / The Metaphor), the other answers what you will have by the end (Overview / The Outcome). Neither panel is optional and neither may repeat the other.
principles.trigger: Auditing the opening of any week page or assignment for whether intent is set before content.
principles.trust_marker: design_principle: dual_framing — flagged when page starts with instructions and no purpose, outcome uses non-observable verbs ('students will understand'), Purpose and Overview say the same thing, or assignment opens with submission logistics.

principles.name: Principle 4 — Consistent Structural Beats
principles.compact_statement: Every week page hits the same sections in the same order (Banner → Core Questions → Purpose → Preparation → Weekly Schedule); every assignment hits its own beats in order (Banner → Purpose+Overview → Instructions Parts → Submission Criteria → Architect's Reflection); every syllabus hits its order. Students develop an automatic navigation schema.
principles.trigger: Auditing whether section order is consistent across weeks and whether the structure holds through the capstone (Weeks 11–13).
principles.trust_marker: design_principle: structural_beats — flagged when section order changes week to week, some weeks have a reflection and others don't, instruction format varies between bullet and prose, or Weeks 11–13 abandon the structure used in Weeks 01–10.

principles.name: Principle 5 — Rubrics as Observable Behaviors, Not Adjectives
principles.compact_statement: Use a 3-level rating scale (Meets / Developing / Does Not Yet Meet Expectations) on every rubric. Each rating has a description (name) and long_description (full sentence describing observable behavior). Meets-level descriptor names the minimum threshold, not aspirational. Include an Organization & Professional Communication criterion on every rubric.
principles.trigger: Auditing any rubric for whether each rating level describes observable behavior rather than a standalone adjective.
principles.trust_marker: design_principle: observable_rubrics — flagged when 5+ rating levels are used, rating descriptors are single adjectives, criteria overlap, no Organization/Communication criterion exists, weights don't match criterion importance, or rubric is shown only at grading time.

principles.name: Principle 6 — Alignment Traceability
principles.compact_statement: You can draw a straight line from any artifact a student touches back to a course-level outcome: Course Outcome → Module Outcome → Assessment → Rubric Criterion → Activity. A broken link anywhere is a latent gap. This operationalizes Designer Thinking's Outcome → Evidence chain at the artifact level.
principles.trigger: Auditing whether the capstone rubric maps back to syllabus course outcomes, and whether module outcomes scaffold up the Bloom taxonomy across the term.
principles.trust_marker: design_principle: alignment_traceability — flagged when syllabus outcomes never reappear in weekly artifacts, rubric criteria measure something the instruction didn't teach, module outcomes use identical Bloom verbs every week (no scaffolding), or a course outcome has no downstream assessment mapped to it.

sources: BYU-Idaho Architects of Learning course (405800) — the course whose subject matter is course architecture and whose own build is the exemplar.
Companion frameworks: hattie_3phase_knowledge.md, cognitive_load_theory_knowledge.md, designer_thinking_knowledge.md, taxonomy_explorer_knowledge.md, toyota_gap_analysis_knowledge.md.
BYUI institutional view: see agents/templates/byui_course_design/ for the 11 HTML component templates and 1 rubric JSON shape that implement these principles.

pre_run_checklist: Each entry in consumed_by[] resolves to an existing agent spec
post_run_checklist: knowledge/course_design_language_knowledge.json exists with shape=identity and principles[] present

changes: Retrofit: JSON companion authored for pre-existing canvas-toolbox knowledge MD.
Classified shape=identity (six named principles, each with trigger and trust marker — exemplar match to behavioral_discipline pattern).
Runtime strategy set to read_at_runtime via selective_load override; body is ~5.7K tokens (either band) but selective audit-task loading defeats embed-cache benefits.
MD body and canvas-toolbox header convention preserved per Retrofit Mode step 3.
